message:
fix(testing): improve test discovery and pattern similarity detection

Recursive Test Directory Scanning (testing/runner.rs):
- When scanning for skill tests, recurse into nested directories
- Previously only scanned immediate children of the tests/ directory
- Now properly discovers tests in subdirectories like tests/unit/,
  tests/integration/, tests/e2e/
- Skip recursing into tests/ directory itself to avoid infinite loops
- Enables organizing skill tests in a hierarchical structure

Pattern Similarity Algorithm Fix (cass/mining.rs):
- Fix false positive matches in patterns_are_similar() for CodePatterns
- OLD BEHAVIOR: Compared first 20 characters of code as a prefix
  - Problem: Two completely different scripts with same imports
    (e.g., "import os\nimport sys\n\nprint('hello')" vs
    "import os\nimport sys\n\nos.remove('/')") would match
  - This could cause dangerous code patterns to be deduplicated with
    benign patterns, hiding security issues
- NEW BEHAVIOR: Normalize by removing all whitespace, then compare
  - Handles formatting differences (tabs vs spaces, line breaks)
  - Requires actual content to match, not just prefix
  - Much stricter comparison prevents false deduplication
- Add test_patterns_are_similar_false_positive_fixed() to verify the
  fix prevents matching code with same imports but different behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
